references.title: A supervised learning framework: using assessment to identify students at risk of dropping out of a MOOC
references.journal: Journal of Computing in Higher Education

extracted.Courses: 8 Editions of a Learn Moodle Course
extracted.Features: [Positive Ratings in Database, Positive Ratings in Forum Posts, Attempted at least one Quizz, Percentage of Quizzes Attempted, Failing a Quizz Attempt]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Students whose enrollment dates are outside course duration were excluded. Same with students with no accesses.
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Accuracy: 0.8881 - MLP, Precision: 0.9151 - MLP, Recall: 0.9531, F1-Score: 0.9337, AUC: 0.7312}
extracted.Comments:
Paper presents a full supervised learning framework that can be integrated in Moodle and is generalizable for multiple different tasks.

Main idea seems to be that the system is meant to be put into production witht he ability of having multiple possible targets.
extracted.Limitations:
Few alternative algorithms

Only uses interaction indicators

annotations.text: Authors develop and present an Analytics Framework for Moodle. Moreover, the authors develop a case study of a MOOC offered at the school that includes 47000 students.
Analytics framework uses a neural network to make predictions after the first, second and third quarters of the course, achiving 88.71% accuracy, 94% F1 Score and 73% AUC.
